skill.name: property-testing-guide
skill.description: Guides Rust developers in writing property-based tests with proptest for algorithms, data structures, parsers, and serialization logic.
skill.summary: Create Rust proptest strategies and invariants that find edge cases automatically.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found documentation-only Rust and TOML examples, with no executable shell commands, cryptographic code, reconnaissance behavior, network access, or prompt injection attempts. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community-sourced instructional content.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the locations are Markdown code fences around Rust and TOML examples. The skill allows only Read and Grep and contains no executable shell instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are Markdown fence delimiters, not Ruby backtick expressions. The surrounding content is instructional Rust testing text with no command execution path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms, but the cited lines contain skill metadata, serialization test examples, and proptest assertions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic API names or algorithms. They are normal property-testing documentation and assertions.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance, but the cited lines are generated test inputs and comments about valid parser behavior. No evidence found of host discovery, file enumeration, environment probing, or network inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside Rust proptest examples and do not instruct the agent to inspect the host system. There is no reconnaissance intent in the surrounding context.

content.value_statement: Rust teams often miss edge cases when tests cover only fixed examples. This skill shows Claude, Codex, and Claude Code how to design proptest properties, strategies, and invariants.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use property-based testing for Rust code.
Suggests roundtrip properties for serialization and encoding logic.
Defines invariants for sorting algorithms and data structures.
Shows how to compare custom implementations with trusted oracles.
Provides proptest strategy examples for domain-specific input generation.
Explains shrinking as a way to find minimal failing cases.
limitations: Focuses on Rust and the proptest crate.
Provides guidance and examples, but does not execute tests.
Examples require adaptation to real project types and APIs.
Does not cover fuzzing engines or non-Rust testing frameworks.

use_cases.title: Test Parser Robustness
use_cases.description: Generate broad input strings and check that parsers return controlled results instead of panicking.

use_cases.title: Verify Data Structure Invariants
use_cases.description: Apply random operation sequences and assert balance, ordering, or consistency after each operation.

use_cases.title: Improve Serialization Coverage
use_cases.description: Create roundtrip properties that verify encoded and decoded values preserve important fields.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Properties
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a Rust function
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust function and suggest property-based tests I can write with proptest. Focus on invariants and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design proptest strategies for this Rust domain type. Include valid ranges, constraints, and invalid cases worth testing.

prompt_templates.title: Replace Example Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these fixed Rust unit tests into property-based tests. Explain each property and why it should always hold.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Algorithm Properties
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Rust algorithm for missing property tests. Include oracle comparisons, invariants, shrinking considerations, and failure cases.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to test a serializer more thoroughly.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a roundtrip property: generate valid values, serialize them, deserialize the result, and assert that the important fields match.

output_examples.input: A maintainer wants confidence in a custom sorting function.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests checking length preservation, sorted order, and equivalence with the standard library sort across many generated vectors.

output_examples.input: A team has a parser that sometimes fails on unusual input.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends generated input strings, a no-panic property, and separate strategies for valid inputs that should parse successfully.

best_practices: Start with properties that describe behavior, not implementation details.
Constrain generators so they create meaningful valid and invalid inputs.
Use oracle comparisons when a simple trusted implementation exists.
anti_patterns: Do not assert that every random input must be valid.
Do not generate only trivial values that repeat normal unit tests.
Do not ignore minimal failing cases reported by shrinking.

faq.question: What language does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Rust projects that use the proptest crate.

faq.question: Can it write complete tests for my project?
faq.answer: It can guide test design and provide examples, but project-specific code must be adapted to your APIs.

faq.question: What kinds of bugs can property tests find?
faq.answer: They can find edge cases in parsers, serializers, algorithms, and data structures that fixed examples often miss.

faq.question: Does this replace normal unit tests?
faq.answer: No. It complements example-based tests by checking broader behavior over generated inputs.

faq.question: Does the skill run proptest for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and test patterns, but it does not execute commands.

faq.question: Is this useful for security testing?
faq.answer: It can improve robustness testing, especially for parsers and input handling, but it is not a full security audit tool.
